summary: The test suite is thorough and well-layered: unit tests (validator.test.ts) exercise every exported function, integration tests (cli.test.ts) verify all four exit codes and both output formats, and end-to-end tests (workflow.test.ts) drive three distinct fixture scenarios through the full act/GitHub-Actions pipeline with exact-value assertions on counts, secret names, and policy verdicts. All stated requirements — classification logic, sorting, config parsing, markdown/JSON rendering, CLI flags, error handling, workflow structure, and actionlint validation — are covered. Rigor is strong: the 0-days boundary (due today treated as warning) is tested, sorting order is verified, empty groups render '_(none)_', and every malformed-config path gets its own test. Gaps are minor: the other critical boundary (exactly warningDays days remaining should be warning, not ok) is not tested; negative rotationDays is untested in the CLI path; and the hardcoded daysUntilRotation values in renderJson/renderMarkdown fixtures could silently drift if a future refactor changes date math. Design is clean — describe blocks map 1:1 to implementation units, typed ActCase/FixtureSecret interfaces make the act harness self-documenting, helper functions (runCli, buildStagingRepo) eliminate boilerplate, and lifecycle hooks (beforeAll/afterAll) handle temp-dir cleanup correctly. The manual hardcoding of computed day offsets (e.g., -138) is a minor brittleness. Overall this is a high-quality, trustworthy suite.
